title: Blechnum microphyllum
taxonomy: Blechnum microphyllum is classified as a species of fern, belonging to the family Blechnaceae. The Blechnaceae family consists of about 120 species of ferns, commonly known as rock ferns or chain ferns. Blechnum microphyllum is a small to medium-sized fern that is native to eastern Australia and New South Wales. It is an epiphytic fern that grows on tree trunks and branches, forming a dense mat-like growth habit. The genus Blechnum is a group of ferns that are characterized by their chain-like fronds and ability to grow in a variety of habitats.

intro: Blechnum microphyllum, also known as the small-leaf chain fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
